verses.text: He sets his face toward the mountains of Israel, where the high places stood, declaring God will bring a sword and destroy them. Idolatry's haunts are doomed. God strikes at the very seats of false worship.

He sets his face toward the mountains of Israel, where the high places stood, declaring God will bring a sword and destroy them. Idolatry's haunts are doomed. God strikes at the very seats of false worship.

He sets his face toward the mountains of Israel, where the high places stood, declaring God will bring a sword and destroy them. Idolatry's haunts are doomed. God strikes at the very seats of false worship.
The altars shall be desolate, the idols broken, the worshippers slain before them, 'and ye shall know that I am the LORD.' The aim of judgment. God acts that His people may know Him truly at last.
The altars shall be desolate, the idols broken, the worshippers slain before them, 'and ye shall know that I am the LORD.' The aim of judgment. God acts that His people may know Him truly at last.
The altars shall be desolate, the idols broken, the worshippers slain before them, 'and ye shall know that I am the LORD.' The aim of judgment. God acts that His people may know Him truly at last.
The altars shall be desolate, the idols broken, the worshippers slain before them, 'and ye shall know that I am the LORD.' The aim of judgment. God acts that His people may know Him truly at last.
Yet He will leave a remnant among the nations, who shall 'remember me' and 'loathe themselves' for their evils. Mercy spares some. Genuine repentance brings a holy self-loathing for past sin.
Yet He will leave a remnant among the nations, who shall 'remember me' and 'loathe themselves' for their evils. Mercy spares some. Genuine repentance brings a holy self-loathing for past sin.
Yet He will leave a remnant among the nations, who shall 'remember me' and 'loathe themselves' for their evils. Mercy spares some. Genuine repentance brings a holy self-loathing for past sin.
He bids the prophet clap and stamp and cry Alas, for the people shall fall by sword, famine, and plague near and far. Grief over judgment. God's messenger mourns even the doom he must announce.
He bids the prophet clap and stamp and cry Alas, for the people shall fall by sword, famine, and plague near and far. Grief over judgment. God's messenger mourns even the doom he must announce.
They shall know the LORD when the slain lie among their idols, and the land is made desolate. The idols prove powerless. What men trusted in cannot save them in the day of wrath.
